title: DTC Growth Marketer
description:
ABOUT THE OPPORTUNITY

We're looking for a hands-on DTC Growth Marketer who will own the full performance marketing stack for a multi-brand premium bedding company selling direct-to-consumer via Shopify. This is not a "manage the agency" role — you will be in the ad accounts daily, making optimization decisions, and directly accountable for CAC, ROAS, and revenue growth.



We're a US-based e-commerce company generating $12M+/year across Amazon, Walmart, and TikTok Shop. Our DTC channel is early-stage (~$19K/month) with massive upside. You will be the person who scales it.



You'll work alongside an AI operations agent that handles analytics, reporting, competitor intelligence, and strategic specs. Your job is execution: turning data-driven strategies into live, profitable campaigns and conversion improvements.



ABOUT INSPIRATEK

About Us

Inspiratek LLC is a US-based e-commerce company generating $18M+ annually on Amazon, with expanding presence on TikTok Shop and DTC Shopify. We operate 5 private label brands with 20+ active products across the portfolio.

What makes us different: we're building one of the most data-driven operations in the private label space. Our team uses advanced analytics, AI-powered operational agents, custom data pipelines, and structured decision frameworks to manage every aspect of the business , from keyword-level PPC decisions to inventory forecasting to competitive intelligence. This isn't a company where you'll be making gut calls. Every decision is backed by data, and every action plan has measurable outcomes.

We're a 27-person distributed team across the world. We operate in a fast-paced, high-autonomy environment where ownership and accountability matter more than hours logged.

Our DTC channel is our strategic growth priority for
. We're building it from the ground up with the right team — not rushing to throw money at ads, but systematically constructing a profitable, data-driven growth engine.



WHAT YOU'LL OWN



Paid Media (Meta + Google) — 50% of your time

• Manage $3K–$15K/month ad spend across Meta Ads and Google Ads (Shopping, Search, PMAX)

• Build and test full-funnel campaigns: awareness ? consideration ? conversion

• Launch 5-10 new creative concepts per month (you brief the concepts, production team builds assets)

• Maintain blended ROAS above 2x (target 3x+), with CAC below $15

• Weekly creative testing: hook variations, UGC vs. static, format experiments

• Real-time optimization: pause underperformers within 48 hours, scale winners with controlled budget increases

• Platform-specific strategy: understand why Meta creative fatigue differs from Google Shopping bid dynamics



CRO & Funnel Optimization — 25% of your time

• Own the conversion funnel: sessions ? PDP view ? add-to-cart ? checkout ? purchase

• Run A/B tests on product pages, landing pages, checkout flow, and trust signals

• Diagnose conversion drops using Shopify Analytics + Microsoft Clarity heatmaps

• Implement and monitor trust-building elements: reviews, guarantees, social proof, comparison tables

• Mobile-first optimization (79% of our traffic is mobile, currently converting at 1.7% vs. desktop 2.2%)

• Target: move store-wide conversion rate from ~1.8% to 2.5%+ within 6 months



 & Retention Support — 15% of your time

• Collaborate with
 specialist (Klaviyo) on campaign strategy and list growth

• Own the connection between paid acquisition and
 capture (popup optimization, lead magnets)

• Monitor LTV:CAC ratio and repeat purchase rates

• Contribute to post-purchase flow strategy (cross-sell timing, winback triggers)



Reporting & Strategy — 10% of your time

• Weekly performance report: spend, revenue, ROAS, CAC, CVR, AOV with WoW comparison

• Monthly channel P&L with contribution margin analysis

• Proactive identification of scaling opportunities and risks

• Work with the AI operations agent to translate analytics into action



WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE (90-DAY SCORECARD)

Month 1 — Foundation

? Full audit of existing Meta + Google Ads accounts completed

? Underperforming campaigns paused, winning audiences/creatives identified

? First 3 creative test concepts launched

? Baseline conversion funnel mapped with drop-off points identified

? Weekly reporting cadence established



Month 2 — Traction

? Meta ROAS above 1.5x (from current 0.98x)

? At least 2 winning ad sets identified and scaling

? First CRO test completed (PDP or landing page)

?
 capture rate improvement measured

? Google Ads Shopping campaigns live and indexed



Month 3 — Scale

? Blended ROAS above 2x

? CAC trending below $20 (toward $15 target)

? Store-wide conversion rate improved 0.3pp+ from baseline

? Proven creative testing velocity: 5+ new concepts per month

? DTC revenue trajectory toward $25K+/month



REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

Must-Have Experience (Non-Negotiable):

• 3+ years managing paid media for DTC/Shopify brands (not just Amazon PPC or agency reporting)

• Hands-on Meta Ads experience: $3K-$50K/month spend, profitable ROAS track record

• Google Ads experience: Shopping, Search, and/or Performance Max campaigns

• Demonstrable results: show us before/after ROAS, CAC, or revenue numbers from a past DTC brand

• Conversion rate optimization: you've improved CVR on product pages or landing pages with data to prove it

• Proficiency with: Meta Business Manager, Google Ads Manager, Google Analytics (GA4), Shopify Analytics

• Strong analytical skills: can calculate ROAS, CAC, LTV, MER, contribution margin without a calculator app

• English fluency: written and verbal, sufficient for creative briefs and strategic communication



Strong Preferences:

• Experience with premium/lifestyle brands (home, bedding, wellness, beauty)

• Klaviyo or
 marketing platform experience

• A/B testing methodology (not just "we tried something" — structured hypothesis ? test ? result)

• Microsoft Clarity, Hotjar, or similar behavioral analytics tools

• Understanding of attribution challenges (platform-reported vs. actual ROAS discrepancy)



Nice-to-Have:

• Experience with Triple Whale, Northbeam, or post-purchase survey attribution

• Shopify theme editing or basic Liquid knowledge

• Video ad editing skills (CapCut, Premiere, etc.)

• Experience in a company running both Amazon + DTC simultaneously (cross-channel dynamics)









WHAT WE PROVIDE

• AI-powered analytics: Our operations agent delivers daily dashboards, competitor intel, creative diagnosis reports, and strategic specs — you execute against clear, data-backed recommendations

• Existing infrastructure: Shopify store live, Meta + Google Ads accounts active, Klaviyo flows built, GA4 configured, competitor tracking system running

• Creative support: Content production team available for ad asset creation from your briefs

• Clear KPIs: No ambiguity about what success looks like — CAC, ROAS, CVR, revenue targets are defined

• Strategic documents: 17 strategic specs already built (creative briefs, PDP optimization specs, funnel analysis,
 flow architecture) — you inherit a playbook, not a blank page

• Growth trajectory: This is a ground-floor DTC buildout for a $12M+ company — the upside for career growth is significant



DEAL-BREAKERS — DO NOT APPLY IF:

? Your experience is primarily Amazon PPC (Sponsored Products/Brands/Display) — DTC paid media is fundamentally different

? You've only worked agency-side reviewing dashboards, not in-platform making daily optimization decisions

? You can't show concrete before/after metrics from a DTC brand you personally managed

? You're looking for a strategy-only role — this is 80% execution, 20% strategy

? You need constant direction — we want someone who sees a 0.98x ROAS and immediately knows the first 5 things to investigate



APPLICATION PROCESS

Send your cover letter to 
 using this subject line format:

title: Website Creator and Data Entry
description:
We are seeking a skilled website creator to develop a simple, efficient web platform for managing field inspection data. The ideal candidate will be responsible for the following:

Website Development: Build a user-friendly website where field inspectors can log in and submit inspection reports. The site should have a simple interface for data entry, including fields for site details, inspection results, photos, and signatures.

Database Management: Design and implement a secure database to store all inspection data. The system should allow for easy retrieval, filtering, and export of reports.

User Accounts: Set up a basic user management system for different levels of access (e.g., inspectors, managers).

Data Entry (Initial Phase): Assist with initial data entry to populate the system with existing site information.

Training & Support: Provide brief training materials or a quick tutorial for field inspectors on how to use the new system and offer limited post-launch support.

title: Social Media Manager
description:
About Sandwicheez

Sandwicheez is a fast-growing San Francisco sandwich chain serving high-quality food in the US. 

We’re hiring a creative, fast-moving Social Media Manager to grow an exciting, fast-growing sandwich brand. You’ll create engaging TikTok and Instagram content, drive audience growth, and turn everyday moments into craveable stories that boost engagement, brand awareness, and real customer traffic.



The Role

We need a creative, growth-obsessed Social Media Manager to turn raw footage from our California shops into viral, search-optimized masterpieces. You are the director—you scout the trends and tell our US-based team exactly what to film.

What You’ll Do

• Content Creation: Plan, script, and edit high-performing TikToks and Reels that hook viewers in 2 seconds.

• Trends: Identify trends in the sandwich and food market and "translate" them for an audience in the US.

• Social SEO: Perform research and write keyword-rich captions to make us the #.

• Graphic Design: Use Canva to design menus, promos, and local event posts.

• Engagement: Manage comments and DMs to build a real community.

Who You Are

• Experienced: 3–6 years managing social for brands (Food/Restaurant is a huge plus) and a graduate with a degree, but preferably related to business and marketing

• Platform Native: You deeply understand TikTok/IG culture and US consumer nuances.

• Fast: You execute quickly without overthinking. We value speed + quality.

• Storyteller: You know a "sandwich build" isn't just food—it's ASMR (Autonomous Sensory Meridian Response), texture, and craving.



Battle-Ready Setup

• PC/Laptop: Min i5 / 8GB RAM (capable of video editing).

• Internet: Reliable 25 Mbps + Backup (Pocket Wi-Fi).

• Power: UPS/Power station backup.

• Schedule: Must have at least 4 hours overlap with Pacific Time (California).

title: Xactimate Estimator / Insurance Restoration Estimator (Full-Time, Remote)
description:
Location: Remote (Philippines Only)

Schedule: Full-Time | U.S. Business Hours (Pacific or Central Time Preferred)

Compensation: $1,200 – $2,000/month (based on experience) + performance bonuses



About Us



We are a fast-growing U.S.-based company specializing in home services (roofing, solar, and restoration). We support sales teams and contractors by providing accurate estimates, strong backend operations, and high-performance systems.



We are looking for a high-quality estimator who understands insurance-based projects and can produce clean, accurate estimates quickly.



Role Overview



You will be responsible for creating detailed, insurance-compliant estimates using Xactimate. You will work closely with our internal team to scope projects, build estimates, and support revisions/supplements.



This is a high-impact role where accuracy and speed directly affect revenue.



Key Responsibilities

-Create detailed estimates using Xactimate

-Review photos, documents, and scopes to identify all necessary line items

-Build accurate scopes for roofing, exterior, and interior damage

-Submit revisions and supplements when needed

-Ensure estimates align with project requirements and sales expectations

-Maintain organized documentation and files

-Communicate with the internal team for updates and clarifications



Required Qualifications

-Experience using Xactimate (REQUIRED)

-Strong understanding of construction or restoration scope (preferred but not required)

-High attention to detail (critical)

-Strong English communication (written and verbal)

-Ability to work during U.S. business hours

-Reliable internet and workspace



Bonus Qualifications (Highly Preferred)

-Experience with PlanSwift and/or Bluebeam

-Background in construction, engineering, or quantity surveying

-Experience with insurance claims and supplements

-Roofing, solar, or restoration industry experience



What We’re Looking For

-Detail-oriented and accurate

-Fast turnaround without sacrificing quality

-Able to follow systems and processes

-Coachable and willing to improve

-Long-term team player



Compensation & Growth

-Competitive base salary based on experience

-Performance bonuses (based on output and quality)

-Opportunity for long-term growth into senior estimator or team lead

-Stable, long-term role with a growing company



How to Apply



To apply, please include:



Your experience with Xactimate (how long, level of expertise)

Any experience with PlanSwift or Bluebeam (if applicable)

Your construction or estimating background (if any)

A short description of a recent estimate you completed

Screenshot or sample of your work (if available)



IMPORTANT: Include the word “ACCURATE” at the top of your application so we know you read this.

title: URGENT HIRING: Social Media Manager (Viral Content & Growth)
description:
We’re looking for a social media native to help manage and scale accounts that generate millions of views every month.



This isn’t a basic “schedule posts and log off” role. It’s for someone who genuinely understands the internet — someone who notices trends early, understands why content performs, and enjoys turning good posts into great ones that reach massive audiences.



If you’re the type of person who scrolls social media and naturally analyzes why something went viral, you’ll feel right at home here.



This is a great opportunity for someone who wants real experience managing high-performing social media accounts, learning viral content strategy, and building a long-term career in the social media and creator economy.



What You’ll Be Doing:



You’ll help manage and optimize multiple social media pages by posting content, monitoring performance, and making smart decisions based on trends and engagement.



Your work will involve researching short-form content trends, reviewing how posts perform, spotting opportunities to improve results, and applying feedback to help our pages continue growing.



You’ll also learn our internal workflows and strategies that help our content consistently reach large audiences.





We’re looking for someone who is:



• Highly reliable and organized

• Comfortable working independently in a remote environment

• Detail-oriented with strong visual judgment

• Naturally curious about why content goes viral

• Familiar with social media trends, formats, and internet culture

• Fluent in English and able to communicate clearly

• Open to feedback and eager to improve



Gen Z applicants or those deeply familiar with Gen Z internet culture are strongly encouraged to apply, but this is not a requirement.



Experience is helpful, but passion, instincts, and work ethic matter more than formal background.



Why This Role Is a Great Opportunity



• Fully remote work

• Paid training and ongoing mentorship

• Performance-based bonuses

• Work on social media accounts that reach millions of views

• Clear path for growth into higher-level social media or strategy roles

• Supportive team environment with no micromanagement



How to Apply

1?? A portfolio or examples of content you’ve worked on (personal projects are welcome)

 subject line should be: SUPERSTAR SMM

We’re looking for people who bring creativity, curiosity, and strong work ethic — not copy-paste applications.



If social media is something you genuinely enjoy and want to turn into a long-term career, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Procurement Admin
description:

We are an Australian-based BPO seeking an experienced Procurement Admin to support our client, an Australian mining company.

mbers with administrative tasks as needed

Systems & Tools You’ll Use

title: Graphic Designer + Content Creator / Wellness Agency
description:
About Us:

The Coupet Collective is a boutique digital marketing agency based in the U.S. that works exclusively with wellness brands — compounding pharmacies, telehealth companies, medspas, fitness tech, supplement brands, and mental health platforms. We’re a small, tight-knit creative team that values high-quality design, clear communication, and real ownership of your work.



We’re looking for a talented Graphic Designer / Content Creator to join us part-time and work directly under our CEO and Lead Brand & Content Strategist. This is a hands-on creative production role — you’ll be executing design projects across multiple wellness clients, not managing strategy. If you love clean, modern design and thrive with clear direction, keep reading.



What You’ll Be Doing:

Designing branded social media graphics (Instagram,
, LinkedIn, TikTok) — static posts, carousels, stories, and short-form video cover images

Creating marketing collateral: one-pagers, sell sheets, PDFs, flyers, and digital brochures

Designing
 graphics and layouts for campaigns and newsletters

Building and formatting presentation decks and client-facing documents

Adapting and resizing assets across platforms and formats

Laying out branded content using established brand guidelines, color palettes, and font systems

Supporting content calendar production by designing batched assets on a weekly basis

Light photo editing and mockup creation as needed

Organizing and maintaining design files in shared folders



What We’re Looking For:

-Required-

Strong proficiency in Canva Pro — this is our primary design tool; you must be advanced, not just familiar

A polished portfolio that demonstrates range: social media content, branded PDFs,
 design, and marketing materials

Clean, modern design sensibility — we lean minimal, editorial, and elevated (not cluttered or over-templated)

Strong typography and layout skills

Ability to follow brand guidelines precisely while still bringing creative ideas to the table

Clear, responsive communication in written English

Reliable internet connection and a distraction-free workspace

Availability to overlap with U.S. Pacific Time for check-ins and feedback



Nice to Have:

Experience designing for wellness, health, beauty, or pharmaceutical brands

Familiarity with Metricool, Later, or other social media scheduling platforms

Basic video editing or motion graphics skills (CapCut, Adobe Premiere, After Effects)

Understanding of social media design best practices: safe zones, aspect ratios, platform specs





Compensation & Growth

This role starts at $7 USD/hour at 8 hours per week. The rate and hours are set — please only apply if you’re comfortable with this structure. That said, we’re actively building our team, and this role is genuinely designed to grow. The right person will have the opportunity to take on more hours, more responsibility, and higher pay as TCC scales.





About the Role

You’ll report directly to our CEO and Lead Brand Strategist. We’ll provide clear briefs, brand assets, and direction — your job is to execute beautifully and efficiently. We move fast, give clear feedback, and respect your time.



A typical week might look like:

Receive a batch brief for a client’s weekly social content (6–10 graphics)

Design a branded PDF or one-pager for a new product or campaign

Create
 graphics for a client newsletter

Resize and adapt existing assets for different platforms

Make revisions based on feedback (usually 1–2 rounds)

Organize and upload final files to shared folders





How to Apply:

Please include the following in your application:



- Your Portfolio



- Include the word “WELLNESS” at the top of your application so we know you read the full posting.

Experience in engineering or construction environments and exposure to payroll or contractor coordination will be an advantage.

title: Video Editor / Animator (Part-Time ? Full-Time Opportunity)
description:
Video Editor / Animator (Part-Time ? Full-Time Opportunity)



Type: Part-Time (20 hours per week)

Schedule: Day Shift

Salary: $7 USD per hour (1,212 USD per month) – paid every Friday

Potential: Opportunity to transition to Full-Time



About Us



We are an Australian AI technology company building innovative tools for legal professionals. Our platform helps lawyers work more efficiently using artificial intelligence.



As we continue to grow, we are expanding our video content production to support educational content, product demonstrations, and marketing campaigns. We’re looking for a creative and reliable Video Editor / Animator who can help bring our ideas to life.



Role Overview



As our Video Editor / Animator, you will transform raw footage and concepts into engaging, professional, and visually compelling videos.



You will work closely with our team to produce content for education, marketing, and product storytelling, helping communicate complex ideas in a clear and engaging way.



We have a structured workflow and feedback system in place. Editors will be expected to document feedback and improvements using a checklist system we provide, helping ensure continuous improvement and consistent production quality across projects.



Key Responsibilities



• Edit and enhance raw video footage into polished final videos



• Create engaging narratives through pacing, transitions, and storytelling



• Add graphics, animations, captions, and visual effects where appropriate



• Improve audio quality through noise reduction, music integration, and voiceovers



• Follow editing guidelines and production checklists provided by the team



• Record feedback and improvement notes into the project checklist system



• Organize video files and assets to maintain a clean production workflow



• Collaborate with the team to deliver content aligned with project goals



• Make revisions and improvements based on feedback



Required Skills



• Proven experience as a Video Editor



• Strong portfolio demonstrating editing and storytelling ability



Proficiency in tools such as:



• Adobe Premiere Pro



• After Effects



• Final Cut Pro



• or similar editing software



Strong understanding of:



• Video formats and resolutions



• Export settings and compression



• Audio editing and sound balancing



• Ability to meet deadlines and manage multiple tasks



• Strong attention to detail



Bonus Skills (Nice to Have)



• Motion graphics / animation experience



• Experience creating educational or SaaS product videos



• Familiarity with AI tools for video production



• Basic graphic design skills



What We’re Looking For



We value editors who are:



• Organized and reliable



• Creative but detail-oriented



• Open to structured workflows and feedback



• Interested in long-term collaboration



This position starts part-time, but strong performers may transition to a full-time role as our content production expands.



Application Requirements



To help us evaluate candidates properly, please include the following in your application:



• Your resume



• A short cover letter explaining your video editing experience



• A link to your portfolio showcasing your past work



• Your LinkedIn profile



• Two professional references we may contact



Please ensure your portfolio links can be viewed in an incognito window (without login access)



Additionally, please include the following details:



• Your internet speed (download and upload)



• Your computer specifications (CPU, RAM, GPU)



• Your backup internet options



• Your backup power solution in case of brownouts or outages



How to Apply



To confirm you’ve read this entire job post, please include the word:



"kangaroo"



at the end of your application.



Join our team and help create high-impact video content for the future of AI in the legal industry.

title: Procurement Category Specialist
description:
Procurement Category Specialist

Full-Time | Remote | Australian Mining Company



We are an Australian-based BPO seeking an experienced Procurement Category Specialist to support our client, an Australian mining company.



This is a full-time (FT) role of 40 hours per week, during Australian business hours. Offering the flexibility of working-from-home (WFH) and a competitive salary package + paid leave + bonuses.



This role is responsible for delivering strategic procurement, sourcing, and category management initiatives, ensuring commercial value, supplier performance, and procurement governance. We’re looking for someone analytical, commercially driven, and experienced in managing tenders, contracts, and supplier relationships.



Key Responsibilities Include



Strategic Sourcing & Category Management

• Plan and implement strategic sourcing, category, and supplier management plans

• Align supplier spend with business objectives and KPIs

• Provide procurement insights and category performance reporting



Tendering & Contract Management

• Manage end-to-end tender processes for high-value procurement activities

• Lead contract negotiations including new contracts, renewals, amendments, and extensions

• Work with legal terms to ensure proper governance and compliance



Supplier & Stakeholder Management

• Build and maintain strong relationships with internal stakeholders and suppliers

• Support vendor management and supplier performance initiatives

• Coordinate supplier prequalification and onboarding



Data Analysis & Reporting

• Perform advanced data analysis to support procurement decisions

• Develop and deliver procurement and category reports

• Provide insights on cost, supplier performance, and savings opportunities



Project Procurement Support

• Support project and equipment procurement activities

• Assist with sourcing, supplier selection, and order placement



Systems & Tools You’ll Use

• Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint)

• Procurement / Supply Chain Systems

• ERP Systems



***** Key Requirements Include ***** MUST HAVES to apply



• 4–5 years of experience in procurement within mining or heavy industry

• Proven experience in strategic sourcing and category management

• Strong experience managing end-to-end tender processes

• Extensive experience negotiating contracts, renewals, and amendments

• Strong commercial acumen and cost analysis capability

• Advanced data analysis and reporting skills (Excel, Pivot Tables, pricing analysis)

• Understanding of supply chain and logistics concepts (domestic and international)

• Relevant tertiary or TAFE qualifications



IDEAL CANDIDATES have

• Strong stakeholder management and relationship-building skills

• Ability to provide leadership and influence decision-making

• Strong communication skills (written and verbal)

• High level of attention to detail and analytical thinking

• Experience supporting mining or large-scale industrial projects



Important – How To Apply



To be considered, please follow these instructions:



Link your CV for us to review.

Provide an overview of your WFH set-up. ( You must have your own work device, fast and reliable internet, back-up internet and power options, co-working space/internet cafe within 20 minutes travel time).

Provide a short outline of your experience in procurement, sourcing, and contract management.

List the systems/tools you’ve used

Provide a short video/voice introduction to yourself.



Applications that do not follow instructions may not be reviewed.



Thank you

title: Creative Content Creator
description:
Role Summary



A multi format Content Creator who can quickly turn rough briefs into engaging content that supports member comms, brand awareness, and lead generation. The priority is ideas + fast execution using available tools/resources (AI, Canva, etc.)—with occasional polished video supported by external services when needed.



Output Expectation

•	Target output: ~5 content pieces per week across formats (social tiles/posts, webinar cutdowns/promos, simple graphics,
 assets, etc.).



Key Responsibilities

•	Produce content across different channels (social,
, webinar/video, event promos, website assets as needed).

•	Translate complex topics (e.g., government/regulatory updates) into clear, member-friendly content quickly.

•	Create and repurpose: social tiles, short-form clips, simple motion, carousels, webinar promos/cutdowns.

•	Support campaigns for training products and events (including “Member Briefing” style promotions).

•	Help build repeatable systems: templates, tone/style guidance, lightweight workflows that increase speed.

•	Iterate quickly with feedback; improve output and process over time.



Required Skills / Working Style

•	Creative, resourceful, and comfortable working from imperfect inputs.

•	Comfortable with “ship fast, improve fast” execution.

•	Tool-flexible (Canva/Figma/Adobe/AI tools like Gemini/Claude, etc.). 

•	Organized and reliable with deadlines.



Success looks like (first 30–60 days)

•	Consistent weekly output with minimal hand-holding.

•	Faster turnaround on member comms and campaign assets.

•	Contributes to broader initiatives like brand refresh and supporting longer-term projects (e.g., website redevelopment) by freeing leadership from day-to-day content production.

title: Executive Assistant
description:
READ THIS FIRST



[SHORT INTRO OF OUR COMPANY]



We are a Singapore based company.



Our main core business involves coaching and training a new generation of financial advisors to reach out to more people, transform more lives and build the business of their dreams.



We have coached more than 3000 Financial Advisors and have more than 8 years under our belt in doing so.



Our clients are mainly in Singapore, Malaysia and Hong Kong and we are on track to reaching out to more of them as we speak.



Right now, We are looking for an Executive Assistant to be part of our company, and to be working directly with the Chief Executive Officer (CEO).



As the Executive Assistant of our company, you will be responsible for coordinating, planning events, communicating to the team and clients, and organising all the backend logistics and operations of the company.



More importantly, your main objective is to buy back time for the CEO.



Your position will play an important and crucial role in the team’s success.





[HOW LIFE IS GONNA LOOK LIKE FOR YOU]



You will be involved in the day to day communications and supporting the CEO directly, assisting in random tasks or any tasks that the CEO requires.





There is no limit to duties an executive assistant can handle, which is why if you are taking on this role, you must have a confident, problem-solving-can-do attitude.



We will not sugarcoat anything but here’s how it is like as well.



You WILL be in a high pressure environment that will force you to step up and grow as an individual.



There will be no bullshit beautiful lies, but rather ugly truth - although necessary - but ugly all the same that will poise you for growth.



While it might be intense, the rewards are great.



Bonuses, employee benefits and perks that will ensure you get well remunerated for your efforts.



The “work hard, play hard and work even harder mantra” is here.



Because we are going to be poised for crazy growth and expansion, you will have to put on your thinking cap, initiative and out of your comfort zone to get things done.





You'll get to attend world-class business, marketing and personal development programs for FREE and rub elbows with renowned Asian entrepreneurs and Financial Advisors.



You’ll be working with an international team, and you’ll even get to hang out with them at least once a year at our annual retreat in an exotic location.





=========

title: Multi-Channel eCommerce Specialist (Amazon, Walmart, eBay, Shopify) – Audit, Fix, and Systemize
description:
We are looking for an experienced eCommerce specialist to help us audit, fix, and improve performance across multiple sales channels, then document processes so our internal team can maintain and scale.



This is not a long-term agency role.

This is a project-based role focused on identifying issues, implementing fixes, and creating systems.



Our business (Capital City Commerce) sells custom and novelty products across:



Amazon

Walmart

eBay

Etsy

Shopify

TikTok Shop



We already have products live and generating sales, but several channels are underperforming or suppressed, and we need an expert to diagnose and fix the root causes.



Scope of Work:

--Phase 1: Audit (High Priority)

     --Review all sales channels and identify key issues impacting performance

     --Prioritize opportunities based on revenue impact and ease of implementation

--Phase 2: Fix & Optimize

     --Walmart

          --Resolve listing quality issues (content, attributes, pricing, shipping)

          --Fix unpublished/suppressed listings

          --Improve listing completeness and compliance

     --Amazon

          --Identify and resolve listing issues (search suppression, inactive listings, etc.)

          --Optimize listings where needed (titles, bullets, backend attributes)

     --eBay

          --Diagnose why store performance is low or suppressed

          --Fix listing structure, item specifics, and visibility issues

     --Shopify

          --Review site and identify opportunities to improve conversion and sales

          --Recommend improvements (UX, product pages, offers, etc.)

     --TikTok Shop

          --Review current setup and provide growth recommendations

--Phase 3: Documentation & Handoff

     --Document key processes and fixes so our internal VA team can maintain and scale

     --Provide clear instructions and SOPs where needed



What We’re Looking For:

--Proven experience managing and optimizing multiple eCommerce platforms

--Strong understanding of:

     --Listing optimization

     --Marketplace policies

     --Catalog structure

     --Conversion fundamentals

--Ability to diagnose problems quickly and prioritize high-impact fixes

--Experience working with existing product catalogs (not just launching new brands)

--Strong communication and ability to explain decisions clearly



What We’re NOT Looking For:

--Agencies with large retainers

--Social media marketers

--“SEO-only” freelancers

--Beginners or general VA-type profiles



Project Details:

--Type: Project-based (with potential for follow-up work)

--Budget: Please include your proposed project-based pricing and timeline based on the scope above.

--Timeline: Ideally completed in 2–4 weeks



To Apply:



Please include:



1. A brief summary of your experience across Amazon, Walmart, eBay, and Shopify

2. 1–2 examples where you:

     a. Diagnosed a struggling store or listings

     b. Fixed the issues and improved performance

3. Your approach to auditing a multi-channel eCommerce business

4. The word “optimize” in the first sentence of your response so we know you read this

title: Content Growth Operator (Marketing Manager – Revenue & Systems Focused)
description:
ABOUT THE ROLE :

We are not looking for a typical social media manager. We are looking for a high-level Content Growth Operator — someone who can turn content, data, and brand into measurable business growth. Your job is not to post content. 



Your job is to build and operate a system that drives: 

? leads 

? sales 

? retention 

? long-term revenue 



This role requires strong thinking, decision-making, and ownership. 



WHO THIS ROLE IS FOR 

This role is for someone who: 

? thinks like a strategist, not just an executor 

? understands how content connects to revenue 

? can analyze data and make decisions independently 

? can lead direction without constant supervision 



WHO THIS ROLE IS NOT FOR 

Do not apply if: 

? you only know how to post or edit content 

? you rely heavily on trends without strategy 

? you need step-by-step instructions for everything 

? you cannot explain how content drives sales 



YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES 

1. Content Strategy & Growth 

? Build and execute a content strategy aligned with revenue goals 

? Identify high-performing content pillars 

? Map content across the full customer journey 



2. Revenue-Focused Marketing 

? Track leads, conversions, and revenue from content 

? Focus on attracting qualified buyers, not just views 

? Align content with offers and sales funnels 



3. Performance Tracking & Optimization 

? Build and manage a weekly performance dashboard 

? Identify what is working, what is not, and why 

? Make data-driven decisions and adjustments 



4. Content Direction (Not Just Execution) 

? Lead ideation and content planning 

? Provide clear direction to editors or creators 

? Extract ideas from the CEO and turn them into scalable content 



5. Systems & Workflow 

? Build SOPs and repeatable systems for content production 

? Create efficient workflows for ideation, posting, and repurposing 



6. Testing & Scaling 

? Run tests on hooks, formats, topics, and CTAs 

? Scale what works and eliminate what does not 



7. Multi-Platform Strategy 

? Understand how different platforms work (Instagram, TikTok, YouTube,
) 

? Adapt content per platform instead of reposting blindly 



REQUIRED SKILLS 

? Strong understanding of content strategy and marketing 

? Ability to connect content to business outcomes and revenue 

? Experience with analytics and performance tracking 

? Strong decision-making and problem-solving skills 

? Ability to think in systems and build processes 

? Strong communication and leadership ability 



WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE 

? Increased qualified leads from content 

? Improved conversion from content to sales 

? Clear weekly insights and action plans 

? A structured content system that runs efficiently 



COMPENSATION 

? Based on experience 

? Performance-based incentives available for strong candidates 



APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS (IMPORTANT) 



To apply, follow these steps exactly: 

1. Send your resume and portfolio.  

2. Send a Loom video (2–5 minutes) answering: 

? How do you turn content into revenue for a service-based business? 

? What metrics matter most to you and why? 

3. Include links to: 

? Brands or accounts you have worked on 

? Specific results you helped achieve 

4. Answer this question in your application: 

? If nothing in our content is performing after 60 days, what would you do? 



Applications without these will not be reviewed. 

You can send your application to: 



FINAL NOTE

title: B2B Outreach Specialist (Part-time  or  full-time  opportunities)
description:
**Please read Before Applying**



**We are seeking experienced B2B cold callers only. Candidates must be confident on the phone and able to generate qualified leads within the first 30 days.***



IHRS is actively seeking a B2B Outreach & Lead Generation Specialist to support our growing business development efforts. This position will also be posted publicly on 
, but we are extending this opportunity directly to you first as a candidate who has already expressed interest in our company.



——————————————

POSITION: B2B OUTREACH & LEAD GENERATION SPECIALIST

——————————————



Engagement Type: Independent Contractor (1099) | Remote



This role is the engine behind IHRS client growth. You will be responsible for identifying prospective healthcare facility clients and organizations, executing outbound outreach, conducting follow-up calls, qualifying leads, and moving potential partners through our pipeline. This is a results-driven position — we are looking for someone who can demonstrate activity, track progress, and deliver outcomes.



What This Role Involves:



• Outbound cold calling and follow-up calls to healthcare facilities, agencies, and organizations

• Lead identification and prospecting using B2B tools, Google research, and provided software

• Following up on active
 campaigns with live phone outreach

• Qualifying leads and logging all activity into our CRM and tracking systems

• Submitting weekly call and lead reports every Friday

• Representing IHRS professionally in every interaction



——————————————

ENGAGEMENT OPTIONS & COMPENSATION

——————————————



We offer two engagement options to accommodate the right candidate:



Part-Time

• 6 hours per day | Monday through Friday

• 30 hours per week

• Base Rate: $500.00/month ( plus an additional $300.00  monthly incentive for every lead that is closed) on top of your base pay)

Hours: 9am -3pm (Eastern Standard Time)



Full-Time

• 7 hours per day | Monday through Friday

• 35 hours per week

• Base Rate: $600.00/month  ( plus an additional $300.00  monthly incentive for every lead that is closed) on top of your base pay)

Hours :9am - 4pm ( Eastern Standard Time)



Performance Incentive (Both Options)

• $300.00 bonus for every lead that converts into a confirmed IHRS client or partner

• This incentive applies from day one and is paid on top of your base rate



——————————————

60-DAY PROBATIONARY PERIOD

——————————————



The first 60 days of engagement serve as a probationary period. At the close of that period, a formal performance review will be conducted. Candidates who demonstrate consistent activity, measurable results, and alignment with IHRS standards will be considered for base pay increases and enhanced incentive structures. We invest in the people who invest in this company.



——————————————

WHO WE ARE LOOKING FOR

——————————————



The right candidate for this role does not just talk about what they can do — they show it. We are specifically looking for someone who brings:



• Demonstrated experience in B2B outreach, cold calling, or lead generation

• The ability to produce measurable results and report on activity with clarity

• Strong verbal communication and professional phone presence

• Reliability, integrity, and a consistent work ethic

• A goal-driven mindset with the discipline to execute daily without micromanagement

• A genuine team-player attitude and desire for long-term growth within the company

• Flexibility and adaptability — IHRS is a growing company and the right person will grow with us



——————————————

HOW TO APPLY

——————————————



If this opportunity interests you, please reply to this
 with the following:



1. Your updated resume or brief overview of your B2B and outreach experience



2. Your preferred engagement option (part-time or full-time)



3. Your availability to start



4. Answer the following question: What makes you the best candidate?



Contact us directly 


 

Website


 



Warm regards,

title: Tiktok Shop  Manager
description:
About Inspiratek

Inspiratek is a U.S.-based e-commerce company generating $16M+ in annual revenue across Amazon, TikTok Shop, and direct-to-consumer channels. We own and operate a portfolio of 5 premium bedding brands sold primarily through Amazon US, with a rapidly growing TikTok Shop channel currently generating ~$2.5M annually.

We are a data-driven, operationally disciplined team of 27+ people. Every decision we make is backed by metrics, every initiative has a clear owner and kill criteria, and every tea
mber is expected to operate with autonomy and accountability.



We are not a startup experimenting with TikTok. We are a profitable, scaling business that needs a proven operator to own and grow our TikTok Shop channel from $2.5M to $5M+ within 12 months.



The Role

You will be the single owner of TikTok Shop operations across our entire brand portfolio. This is not a content creation role or a virtual assistant position — this is a channel management role with full P&L responsibility. You will manage the affiliate/creator ecosystem, oversee GMV Max ad campaigns, coordinate product launches on TikTok, and drive profitable revenue growth.

You report directly to the CEO. You will work cross-functionally with our Amazon operations team, creative/product development team, logistics team, and external agencies. You will be evaluated on revenue growth, profitability, creator activation rates, and content velocity — not activity metrics.



What You Will Own

1. TikTok Shop Strategy & Revenue Growth

• Develop and execute the TikTok Shop strategy across all brands (currently 5 bedding brands)

• Own the channel P&L — you are responsible for GMV, ROAS, net margin, and CAC

• Set monthly/quarterly revenue targets and build the execution plan to hit them

• Identify and capitalize on TikTok trends, seasonal opportunities, and promotional events (e.g., Super Brand Day, Mega Sales)

• Manage product catalog on TikTok Seller Center — listings, pricing, inventory coordination, promotions

• Coordinate with Amazon ops to prevent cross-channel cannibalization and align launch timing

2. Creator & Affiliate Program Management

• Build and scale a high-performing creator network (target: 200+ active creators within 6 months)

• Recruit creators through Affiliate Center, manual outreach, and community building

• Develop tiered commission structures that reward performance without destroying margin

• Manage product sampling/seeding program — budget allocation, creator selection criteria, fulfillment coordination

• Track creator activation rates (% of seeded creators who actually post) and implement re-engagement sequences

• Build a creator community (Slack/
) with content calendars, best practices, and incentive programs

• Coach creators on content optimization: hooks, CTAs, product demonstration, FTC compliance

3. GMV Max & Paid Advertising

• Manage GMV Max campaigns with budgets of $500–$5,000+/day across multiple brands

• Structure campaigns by product, objective, and creative type

• Optimize for ROAS, not just GMV — every dollar spent must be justified by margin contribution

• Identify and amplify top-performing organic creator content via Spark Ads

• A/B test creative angles, hooks, and CTAs at scale

• Know when to scale, when to pause, and when to kill — with data to support every decision

4. Performance Analytics & Reporting

• Build and maintain weekly performance reports covering: GMV, orders, AOV, ROAS, CAC, creator posting frequency, content velocity, affiliate commission costs, net margin

• Identify trends before they become problems — declining CVR, creator churn, rising CPA, content velocity drops

• Calculate true profitability per product on TikTok (revenue minus COGS, commissions, ad spend, sampling costs, fulfillment, returns)

• Present data-driven recommendations to leadership with clear trade-offs and financial impact

5. Cross-Functional Coordination

• Align TikTok launches with Amazon product launches and inventory planning

• Work with creative team on product photography, video assets, and listing content optimized for TikTok

• Coordinate with logistics on TikTok-specific fulfillment (FBT, seller shipping, sample shipments)

• Manage relationships with external agencies or freelancers supporting TikTok operations



Requirements

Must Have (Non-Negotiable)

• 2–4+ years of direct TikTok Shop management experience as a seller (not just as a creator or agency)

• Proven track record of managing $50K+/month in TikTok Shop GMV with documented results

• Hands-on experience with TikTok Seller Center, Affiliate Center, and GMV Max campaign management

• Demonstrated ability to recruit, manage, and scale a creator/affiliate network of 50+ active creators

• Strong analytical skills — comfortable building reports, calculating margins, and making data-driven decisions

• Experience managing GMV Max campaigns with budgets of $500+/day and achieving ROAS > 3.0x

• Financial literacy — understand unit economics, contribution margin, break-even ROAS

• Native or near-native English proficiency

Strong Preference

• Experience in home goods, bedding, lifestyle, or wellness categories on TikTok Shop

• Experience managing multiple brands on TikTok simultaneously

• Familiarity with TikTok Shop analytics tools (Kalodata, FastMoss, or similar)

• Experience with product seeding/sampling programs at scale (100+ samples/month)

• Understanding of Amazon operations (helpful for cross-channel coordination)

• Experience with Live Shopping on TikTok

Nice to Have

• Experience with TikTok Shop API integrations

• Content creation skills (CapCut, video editing)

• Experience scaling a TikTok Shop channel from

title: Senior Video Editor (US-Based Brand | PST Schedule)
description:
Application Requirements:

1. Use at least 10AP for your application.

2. Include "Senior Video Editor Pro" in your subject line.



Note: Applications missing either requirement will not be considered.

_________________________________________________________



About Veritas Vans

Veritas Vans builds the most trusted accessories for Starlink Mini and Starlink Standard. Our mission is to give people the freedom to work, explore, and stay connected anywhere. From Baja coastlines to deep forest trails, our customers rely on our gear for capability, protection, and unlimited connection.



Your role is to help visually tell this story across all platforms.



Role Overview

We are hiring a Senior Video Editor who can own our long-form YouTube content, high-retention short-form content, and graphic design needs. You must be highly experienced, creative, fast, and able to work autonomously within a growing brand.



Responsibilities



Video Editing – Long Form

- Edit 6–15 minute YouTube videos with strong retention, pacing, and narrative clarity.

- Add b-roll, overlays, captions, music, and brand-consistent graphics.

- Build engaging openings with proven YouTube hook methods.



Video Editing – Short Form

- Execute the Hook ? Retain ? Reward method for Reels, TikTok, and Shorts.

- Add dynamic subtitles, zooms, cuts, 
 inserts, and pacing adjustments.

- Produce scroll-stopping, high-retention content.



Graphic Design & Photo Editing

- Create YouTube thumbnails and graphic assets.

- Design text treatments, overlays, and promotional visuals.

- Edit lifestyle and product photos for multi-platform use.



Brand Storytelling

- Align all edits with our brand themes: freedom, connection, capability, control.

- Contribute creative ideas and concepts for future video content.



AI-Driven Editing & Workflow Optimization

- Use AI tools (ChatGPT, Adobe AI, Topaz, Descript, etc.) as a core part of your editing workflow.

- Generate high-performing hooks, captions, and on-screen text callouts using AI.

- Use AI to research and improve storytelling, pacing, and content structure.

- Continuously explore and implement new AI workflows to improve output quality, speed, and efficiency.

- Leverage AI to enhance both creative output and production throughput, not just assist basic tasks.



Cross-Platform Optimization

- Edit in multiple aspect ratios: 16:9, 9:16, 4:5, 1:1.

- Prepare final deliverables in organized, structured folders.

- Optimize content for YouTube, Instagram, TikTok,
.



Collaboration

- Work closely with the content team and leadership.

- Follow SOPs and meet deadlines consistently.

- Join scheduled calls proactively (no reminders required)

- Be punctual and prepared for feedback sessions



Work Discipline & Delivery Standards

- Be actively available and responsive during working hours (PST schedule).

- Address tasks, revisions, and team communication within working hours.

- Meet deadlines consistently and communicate early if delays occur.

- Submit deliverables early in the workday whenever possible to allow time for review and revisions.



Quality & Output Standards

- Deliver edits that are near publish-ready on first submission.

- Ensure drafts require minimal revisions and are not incomplete or partially executed.

- Perform a full self-review before submission to ensure:

- all feedback is applied

- no new issues are introduced

- output meets brand and quality standards

- Maintain consistently high production quality across all deliverables.



Note:

Include the phrase “Senior Video Editor Pro” in your subject line. Applications missing this phrase will not be considered. Use at least 5AP for your application.



Required Skills



- Ability to work graveyard shift (PST hours)

- 3–5+ years professional video editing experience

- Expert in Adobe Premiere Pro

- Strong skills in After Effects, Photoshop, Illustrator, or Figma

- Proven experience editing YouTube content and high-performing short-form videos

- Strong understanding of retention, pacing, story structure, and attention engineering

- Experience creating high-converting thumbnails

- Strong understanding of modern content trends across YouTube, Instagram, and TikTok

- Ability to edit lifestyle, outdoor, product, and UGC-style content



AI & Workflow Skills (Required)

- Strong proficiency in using AI tools (ChatGPT, Adobe AI, etc.) within editing workflows

- Ability to generate hooks, captions, and on-screen text using AI

- Experience using AI to improve storytelling, pacing, and video performance

- Ability to research and implement new AI workflows to improve efficiency and output quality



Work Ethic & Discipline

- Highly reliable, responsive, and accountable

- Strong attention to detail and ability to self-review work

- Ability to follow feedback precisely and minimize repeated revisions



Bonus Experience

- Motion graphics or animation skills.

- Experience in e-commerce, outdoor brands, or creator channels.

- Understanding of YouTube SEO (titles, thumbnails, keywords).

- Ability to generate ideas for hooks, storylines, and visual treatments.



What We Offer

- Long-term employment with growth potential.

- Competitive salary based on experience.

- Consistent weekly workload.

- Direct impact on a fast-scaling global brand.

- Creative freedom and an opportunity to lead our video direction.



How to Apply



Submit the following:

- Short explanation of why you’re the right fit

- Brief explanation of how you use AI in your editing workflow

- 1 example where you improved video performance (retention, CTR, etc.)

mber, and present monthly quality reports to leadership.

Lead root cause analysis when content errors reach clients; assign corrective actions and

title: Sales Development Representative
description:
Sales Representative - Church App Platform

Who We Are

Vinebranch Labs is a nonprofit on a mission to help churches stay connected with their people beyond Sunday.

We are building a platform that gives every church their own app. Not a generic church management tool. A real, branded app for their church that keeps their congregation engaged throughout the entire week.

The centerpiece is something no one else has built: a personalization engine that takes a pastor's weekly sermon and turns it into daily devotionals, Bible study prompts, and scripture readings for the whole congregation, automatically, every week. The pastor preaches on Sunday, and their message reaches their people every single day after that, without the pastor doing any extra work.

On top of that, the app includes a prayer wall where members can share requests and pray for each other, event listings, community messaging, giving, small group tools, and more. Everything a church needs to stay connected in one place.

Most churches today have nothing. Maybe a
 group and a website from 2015. Their people walk out on Sunday and don't engage with the church again until next week. We are changing that.

Our founder previously built a SaaS company with 70+ employees and over $6M in annual revenue. We know how to build and scale software businesses. 

But this time, we are doing it as a nonprofit, with all profits going toward spreading God's Word and supporting Christian causes around the world. 

This is a ministry, not just a tech company.

Why This Matters

Think about what churches are dealing with right now.

Pastors spend hours every week preparing a sermon. They pour their heart into it. They deliver it on Sunday. And then on Monday, most of their congregation has already moved on. The message fades. People drift. By the time next Sunday comes around, they have to start from scratch.

What if that sermon could keep working all week? 

What if every member got a daily devotional on their phone, tied directly to what their pastor taught on Sunday? 

What if the church had a place where people could pray for each other, sign up for events, connect with their small group, and give, all in one app with their church's name on it?

That is what we are building. And we are going to bring it to thousands of churches.

We are starting with Churches in the US and Australia, where most churches have no app at all. The competition is thin, the need is real, and the price point is low so we plan to make it an easy decision for any pastor. We take their sermon, show them what their app could look like, and let the product sell itself.

This is showing a pastor something that makes them say, "I need this for my church."

The Role

We are hiring sales representatives to help us bring this product to churches, starting in the US or Australia.

Your job is simple: reach out to pastors and church leaders, introduce the platform, show them a personalized preview of what their church's app would look like, and help them get started.

What you will do day to day:

Research and build lists of churches to contact

Reach out to pastors and church leaders via
 and phone

Walk them through a demo of the platform and show them what their church's app would look like

Follow up with interested churches and guide them through to signing up

Track your outreach and pipeline activity in a CRM

Share feedback from conversations with pastors so we can improve the product

Build relationships with pastors who can refer other churches to us

What we are looking for:

B2B sales experience, ideally in SaaS or outbound sales (ideally 2 years+ sales experience)

Strong English communication skills (you will be speaking with pastors daily)

A practicing Christian who attends church regularly and genuinely understands church life

Self-motivated and comfortable working independently in a remote role

Comfortable with cold outreach 
 and phone)

Honest, warm, and able to build trust quickly

Why this role is different:

This is not selling software to a corporation that does not care. Every deal you close means a real church community gets a tool that keeps them connected to God's Word throughout the week. You are directly helping pastors extend their ministry. You will hear pastors get excited when they see what their app could look like. That is the daily experience of this job.

You are also getting in on the ground floor. This is an early-stage company with a founder who has already built and scaled a business before. If you are the type of person who wants to be part of building something meaningful from the beginning, not just filling a seat at a big company, this is your opportunity.

Compensation:

Base salary plus commission on every church you close. 

We care about results, not when you are sitting at a desk.



Your Application

Thank you for taking the time to apply. We want to get to know you beyond a resume, which is why we are asking you to answer a few short audio questions.

Please be yourself. We are not looking for perfect answers. We are looking for people who are genuine, who care about this mission, and who can communicate warmly and clearly, because that is exactly what this job requires every day.

God Bless, and we look forward to hearing from you.

Thank you!

title: Logistics & Supply Chain Lead
description:
COMPANY SNAPSHOT

We’re a growing e-commerce brand focused on bedding and textile products, selling primarily through Amazon. Our catalog spans approximately 20 product lines with deep variation across colors and sizes. We’re in a high-growth phase with strong product-market fit, and we’re building the team to scale significantly over the next 12 months.



WHY THIS ROLE EXISTS

Our logistics department has more work than bandwidth. Recurring operations, analytical projects, and new process development all compete for the same limited hours, and two of our four strategic pillars—Supply Chain & Supplier Management and 3PL & FBA Operations—have minimal formal coverage today.

Without this hire, significant financial exposure in storage fees and surcharges continues to compound, supplier negotiation and freight optimization remain unaddressed, and the forecasting process can’t mature into a cross-functional system. This role exists to take ownership of processes and planning so the department can operate across all four pillars simultaneously—and so the financial leaks stop growing.



 WHAT YOU’LL OWN

Inventory Intelligence & Demand Planning — Own days-of-supply targets, safety stock methodology, and the forecasting process across all SKU tiers. Keep hero SKUs within target supply windows. Build a cross-functional forecasting process that pulls from PPC, deals, and channel data to continuously improve accuracy.

Supply Chain & Supplier Management — Build this function from the ground up. Establish the PO lifecycle from issuance to receipt, create supplier scorecards tracking lead time, defect rates, and cost per unit, coordinate QC, and manage freight. Define what good looks like and build toward it.

3PL & FBA Operations — Manage 3PL relationships and FBA inbound logistics. Define and track 3PL SLAs, plan inbound shipments to avoid surcharge threshold breaches, build a reimbursement recovery process, and monitor FC check-in and receiving times.

Cost Optimization & Storage Governance — Drive systematic fee mitigation across long-term storage fees, storage utilization surcharges, shipping rates, and landed cost. Own the financial exposure dashboard. Make sure we never get surprised by a cost cliff we should have seen coming.

Team Development & Process Formalization — Document SOPs for all recurring processes, establish cadences that run without prompting, cross-train to eliminate single points of failure, and develop the team as it grows. Build a department that works even when you’re not in the room.



WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

In the first 90 days:

You’re independently running the weekly KPI cadence and department check-in

You’ve taken ownership of the Supply Chain and 3PL pillars, with current-state processes documented and first improvements underway

Storage fee exposure and surcharge risk are under active, systematic management—not reactive firefighting

By 12 months:

All four strategic pillars are staffed and operating with documented SOPs

KPIs are trending positively across days of supply, storage fees, surcharges, forecast accuracy, and landed cost

The department can absorb demand fluctuations without crisis-mode escalation, and leadership oversight has shifted from weekly hands-on involvement to monthly strategic reviews



WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Requirements:

Strong proficiency in written and spoken English

4–6 years in logistics or supply chain roles, with at least 2 years owning outcomes (not just executing tasks) in a leadership or senior IC capacity

Hands-on Amazon FBA experience—you understand long-term storage fee mechanics, storage utilization surcharges, inbound shipment planning, and fee structures at a tactical level

Experience managing at least one 3PL relationship end-to-end (contracts, SLAs, cost benchmarking, receiving coordination)

Proven track record of building processes from scratch in a resource-constrained environment—not just optimizing what someone else built

Experience managing or building a small logistics team—you’ll be leading existing operators and growing the function over time

Advanced Excel / Google Sheets fluency—this is a spreadsheet-heavy environment and you’ll be building models, dashboards, and trackers from day one

Financial acuity—you naturally translate logistics decisions into dollar impact and use that to drive prioritization

You’ll thrive here if you:

Treat the department’s financial exposure like it’s your own money—you don’t wait to be told something is a problem

Instinctively create repeatable processes rather than solving the same problem manually each time

Can look at a list of urgent items and pick the ones that actually matter this week, then communicate clearly about what’s being deferred and why



THIS ISN’T FOR YOU IF…

Your strength is strategy and frameworks but you’re not excited about personally pulling data and building spreadsheets—at least until the team grows

You need heavy top-down direction to know what to work on, or you need perfect information before you’re willing to make a call

Your experience is entirely in enterprise or Fortune 500 logistics without e-commerce or Amazon exposure

You see a small, early-stage team and think “that’s beneath me” rather than “that’s a chance to build something”





8. WHAT WE OFFER

Work Arrangement

Fully Remote. Company operates on EST hours with flexible scheduling.

Growth Opportunity

This is a ground-floor leadership role. You’ll build the logistics function and grow the team beneath you. As the operation scales, so does your scope and impact.

Team & Culture

You’ll join a lean, high-autonomy team where results matter more than process theater. The CEO provides strategic direction and removes structural blockers; you run the operation. Expect direct communication, low ego, and a bias toward action.



HOW TO APPLY

To apply, submit your resume along with a brief response (3–5 sentences) to the following question:

Describe a time you inherited a logistics or supply chain function with no documented processes and built the operating system from scratch. What did you prioritize first, and why?

This question is your first filter. Candidates who can answer it with specifics—not generalities—are the ones we want to talk to.

title: Digital Marketing Specialist – Ecommerce (Long-Term, Full-Time)
description:
I own an established Australian ecommerce business that manufactures and ships products to customers across Australia, New Zealand, the UK, and the USA. We've been operating for 16 years and have over 50,000 customers.



I'm looking for a skilled Digital Marketing Specialist to take full ownership of our online marketing. This is not a VA role where you'll be doing a bit of everything. This is a dedicated marketing position where you'll own our Google Ads, our Meta advertising, and our digital asset creation.



This is a long-term role with a real business. I'm not looking for someone for a project — I'm looking for someone who wants to grow with us. If you perform well, there is a genuine opportunity to take on more responsibility, manage additional clients, and increase your earnings significantly over time.



Important: Read This Before Applying

I need to be direct about a few things.

Be honest about your skills. If you're excellent at Google Ads but have limited Meta experience, tell me that. If your design skills are stronger than your analytics, say so. I can work with someone who has gaps — I cannot work with someone who pretends they don't.



I have full visibility across every platform. I can see what's been done, what's been changed, and what results came from it. I don't say this to be intimidating — I say it so we start this relationship with complete transparency. Honesty is the most important quality I'm looking for.



How We'll Work Together

Even though you'll be working remotely, you'll be part of our team, not just a freelancer in the background. Here's how we'll stay connected:

You must be contactable at all times during your agreed work hours via Slack, WhatsApp, or similar. If I message you, I expect a response within minutes, not hours.

Weekly Zoo
eting with me and our customer support manager Kara to review progress, discuss the business, and plan ahead.



I will share our business with you through video walkthroughs of our warehouse, products, and operations so you understand what we make, how we ship, and who our customers are. You need to know this business inside out, even from a distance.



Screen sharing and virtual training sessions as needed so we can collaborate on campaigns and strategy in real time.



Monthly reporting — you present your results, what worked, what didn't, and what you want to try next.



This isn't a set-and-forget arrangement. You'll feel like part of the team because you will be part of the team.



What You'll Be Doing

Google Ads (Core Focus):

Manage and optimise Google Ads campaigns (Search, Shopping, Performance Max)

Monitor spend, adjust bids, test ad copy, and improve ROAS

Analyse campaign data and make decisions based on real numbers

Provide clear reporting with actionable recommendations

Meta Advertising 
 & Instagram):

Create, manage, and 
 paid
 and Instagram advertising campaigns

Audience building, retargeting, creative testing, and budget management

Coordinate organic social media content alongside paid campaigns

Digital Asset Creation:

Create social media graphics, ad creatives, banners, and promotional images

Produce visual content for campaigns and promotions

Comfortable with Canva and/or Adobe tools



AI Tools:

I use Claude (by Anthropic) extensively to analyse data, generate content ideas, and solve business problems. I'm looking for someone who either already uses AI tools like Claude, ChatGPT, or similar in their workflow — or is genuinely excited to learn. If you're already using AI to improve your marketing work, tell me about it in your application. This is a genuine advantage.



Reporting:

Monthly report covering traffic, conversions, ad performance, and social growth

Identify what's working, what's not, and what to try next

Be prepared to present and discuss your results on our weekly Zoom call



What I'm Looking For



MUST HAVE:

At least 2 years of hands-on experience managing Google Ads campaigns with real, measurable results

Experience running Meta 
/Instagram) paid advertising

Ability to create digital assets — graphics, ad creatives, social media content



Strong written AND spoken English — you'll be on weekly video calls with us and creating ad copy for an Australian audience

Self-motivated and organised — you manage your own day without being told what to do



Honest and transparent — if you don't know something, say so. If something isn't working, tell me before I find out myself



Reliable internet connection, webcam, and a quiet workspace for video calls

Available Monday to Thursday, with hours overlapping Australian business hours (AEST)



Contactable during work hours — responsive on messaging platforms



NICE TO HAVE:

Experience with BigCommerce or Shopify

Understanding of SEO and Google Search Console

Experience using AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, etc.) for marketing or data analysis

Experience with Google Analytics 4

Experience working with ecommerce or physical product businesses

Experience working with Australian businesses

Personal interest in backyard chickens, poultry keeping, or small-scale farming — our customers are passionate about their flocks, and understanding that world makes a real difference to the quality of content and campaigns you'll produce

How to Apply

Do NOT send me a generic application. I will ignore it.

Answer these five questions:

Show me a Google Ads campaign you managed. What was the business, what did you do, and what were the results? Include real numbers. If you also have Meta Ads results to share, include those too.

If I gave you access to an ecommerce store with 50,000 customers, declining traffic, and a Google Ads account that needs work — what would you do in your first 30 days? Be specific.

What digital assets have you created? Send me examples — ad creatives, social media graphics, banners, anything. A portfolio link or screenshots are fine.

Be honest: what are you strongest at, and where are your gaps? I'd rather know now than find out later.

Do you have any personal experience with backyard chickens, poultry, or small-scale farming? This isn't a dealbreaker, but it's a genuine advantage for this role.

If you answer these five questions well and honestly, you'll get a video interview. If you send me a copy-paste application, you won't hear back.

This is a genuine long-term opportunity with a real business that's been running for 16 years. I'm looking for someone who wants to be part of building something — not just doing tasks.

Organize
s, documents, and digital files

Light personal admin support (no financial decision-making)







?? All financial actions require approval



? Required Qualifications

?? Strong organization and attention to detail



?? Experience supporting a business owner/executive

? Nice to Have (Bonus)
